page: Amazon Web Services r6id.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by third-generation Intel Xeon Scalable 8375C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 1024 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a resource ratio of 8 GB of RAM per physical core. The hardware configuration includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, operating under the Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark testing, the r6id.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for both stress-ng multi-core CPU and Passmark CPU evaluations. Memory performance is strong, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 334.55 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 135.74 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations both rank in the top 25%, with Redis handling 106.91 million operations per second. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall within the average tier, indicating that workloads benefit most from parallelized execution rather than single-threaded speed.

The inclusion of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage alongside a substantial memory footprint provides high resource density, making this instance highly cost-efficient for data-intensive applications that would otherwise require external block storage. The server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, real-time analytics, caching layers, and enterprise applications. The lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, though it maintains strong performance for CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 1024 GB of DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 50 Gbps baseline network speed. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities. The high-density memory and local storage configuration make it a cost-effective choice for memory-intensive workloads without requiring external storage overhead. This server is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, real-time data analytics, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU performance for databases and analytics.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe storage and top-tier multi-core processing for demanding database and analytical workloads.

1024 GB of DDR4 memory for memory-intensive applications
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
